name: Frances Bissell's Low-Fat Christmas Pudding
recipeIngredient: 230 g (8 oz, 4 cups) fresh wholemeal breadcrumbs
60 g (2 oz, 3/4 cup) crumbled almond macaroons or amaretti
60 g (2 oz, 1/2 cup) ground or flaked almonds
2 tbsp orange marmalade or candied orange peel
6 tbsp or 1 miniature bottle of cognac
140 ml (5 fl oz, scant 2/3 cup) fortified muscat wine, port, Marsala, or rich oloroso sherry
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, mix all the dry ingredients thoroughly.
2. In another large bowl, blend marmalade, orange juice, eggs, cognac (or cognac mixture), and wine until well combined and frothy.
3. Pour the liquid mixture over the dry ingredients and stir until moist. Cover and let stand for at least a couple of hours, preferably overnight, to allow spice flavors to develop.
4. Grease the pudding basin and spoon in the mixture. Fill the basin up to 1.25 cm (1/2 inch) from the rim.
5. Place a square of greaseproof or waxed paper over the top of the basin (oil it if desired).
6. Steam the pudding in boiling water for 5 hours.
7. Allow the pudding to cool completely before wrapping it, still in its basin, in fresh greaseproof paper plus a layer of foil. Store in a cool, dark place.
8. On Christmas Day, steam the pudding for an additional two hours. Serve with creme fraiche instead of brandy butter.
